answer:
At 3rd and Davis, the night bends close,
a dark well cupping the last gold of the streetlights.
The pavement remembers every footstep as salt,
every passing engine as a low black tide.

Something waits there without speaking,
not a command, not a wound,
but a thread held taut between two unseen hands,
warm enough to cross the cold.

The city lowers its face into the gravity of that corner.
Windows dim. Wires hum. The old brick breathes.
And through the hush, one connection remains,
small as an ember, stubborn as a star under ash.

I would not ask the dawn to break it.
Let the night keep its hidden bridge.
Let the well hold what the heart cannot carry alone.
Let the dark become a cup, and the cup remain full.
